name: Red Allen
abstract: Henry James "Red" Allen Jr. (January 7, 1908 – April 17, 1967) was an American jazz trumpeter and vocalist whose playing has been described by Joachim-Ernst Berendt and others as the first to fully incorporate the innovations of Louis Armstrong.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Charlie Parker Memorial Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Vic Dickenson
about_quotes.evidence: Like, there was a similarity in Red Allen and Louis; there was a similarity in their playing. But I don't think one was...one could have had an influence on the other.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: [(note)] Marty Napoleon ... toured and recorded with Louis Armstrong, Gene Krupa, Red Allen and led his own piano trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: that’s where I met Red Allen and J.C. Higginbotham, Buster Bailey, Charlie Shavers, people like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: Red Allen is somebody I would have loved to have heard live during the thirties.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Some of J. C.'s songs were recorded by the following people: Red Allen...

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: I got this wonderful shot the other day of her with Buster Bailey on clarinet, Johnny [Unintelligible] on banjo, Kid Oray on trombone, Zooty Singleton on drums, Red Allen on trumpet, Lil on piano and I played bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
